name: Auto-archive payment invoices
description: When a new email lands in the configured inbox, an AI agent decides whether it's a payment invoice we received. If yes, the workflow files the email body, a parsed JSON summary, and every attachment into archive/payments/<year>/<month>/<day>/<vendor>/ on local disk. Use as a starting point — point it at your own inbox, swap the agent's prompt to match your accounting categories, and customise the archive path with whatever folder convention your team prefers.

nodes.name: classifyEmail
nodes.description: Ask the InvoiceClassifier agent (see README) whether this email is a payment invoice and extract the structured fields we'll need to file it.

nodes.name: guard
nodes.description: Skip the rest of the flow when the classifier says this email is NOT a payment invoice. Downstream nodes cascade-skip via Daisy's executeIf machinery, so non-invoice mail is a no-op end to end.

nodes.name: buildPath
nodes.description: Slice the ISO-8601 date string into year/month/day components and sanitise the vendor name for filesystem use. Pure FEEL — no glue node needed.

inputs.expression: { year: substring(input.date, 1, 4), month: substring(input.date, 6, 2), day: substring(input.date, 9, 2), vendor: replace(classification.result.vendor, "[^A-Za-z0-9._-]", "_") }

nodes.name: saveBody
nodes.description: Write the email body as a plain-text file in the per-day folder. Daisy creates the parent directories on demand.

nodes.name: saveMetadata
nodes.description: Sidecar JSON with the parsed invoice fields — invoice number, amount, currency, paymentDate. Downstream reconciliation jobs can read this without re-parsing the email.

nodes.name: saveAttachments
nodes.description: Fan out one write per attachment. The batch context exposes ${item} (the current attachment) and ${index}. Email attachments come through as base64-encoded content, so we pass encoding=base64 to file.write.
